behavior_summary: Provides NPC (guardsman) responses to being looked at and used (talked to), manages area-trigger resurrection/cleanup, and runs a patrol/movement helper.
GUARD3::look: when the player inspects the guard, the script checks if the NPC is dead and barks an appropriate short description ('dead guardsman' vs 'guardsman').

U8P_GUARD3::enterFastArea: on area entry the script will revive and reposition a dead guard (clearing dead/in-combat flags and teleporting) or, if not dead, perform area cleanup and spawn a patrol helper U8P_GUARD3::077B.
U8P_GUARD3::leaveFastArea: cleans up/unregisters area resources related to the fast-area trigger.

U8P_GUARD3::use: implements a dialog flow (greeting, question list, replies) that reads and updates per-guard flags (guard3Met, guard3Pissed) and reads world-state flags (hydrosFree, hasHeart, pyrosFree) to choose warning lines about Titans/undead; it also spawns animation/movement helper methods for follow-up behavior.
U8P_GUARD3::schedule: constructs timing/config values and spawns scheduled helper methods to drive periodic guard behavior; behavior may change depending on which method spawns succeed.
U8P_GUARD3::077B: a script-driven patrol/movement loop that issues pathfind requests and spawns short animation/movement METHOD::091C sequences interleaved with small waits.
notable_control_flow: Look -> conditional branch on Npc::isDead(process) to choose a 'dead' vs 'alive' bark.
enterFastArea -> if Npc::isDead(process) then clear dead/in-combat and teleport; else free area info and spawn the patrol helper U8P_GUARD3::077B.

use -> linear greeting then spawn METHOD::0BCF and a short delay, tally world flags (hydrosFree, hasHeart, pyrosFree) into a local counter to select a warning line, then present an ask-list to the player and branch on the returned string to multiple canned replies; updates guard3Met and guard3Pissed based on choices and may spawn METHOD::1090 as a follow-up.
schedule -> builds timer/config locals and spawns METHOD::143A; depending on the returned pid it may spawn METHOD::133F instead (i.e., alternate scheduled callbacks).
077B -> small byte-driven loop controlling calls to Npc::pathfind and a sequence of spawned METHOD::091C actions with waits, implementing patrol steps.
open_questions: Exact dialog text variants (full strings for all warning/answer lines) and their mapping to each world-flag combination are inferred but not exhaustively enumerated.
Precise timing values and meanings of schedule locals (BP-07h / BP-05h / BP-03h / BP-01h) used to configure METHOD::143A/METHOD::133F are not explicit in the extraction.
Behavioral detail of METHOD::* helpers (0BCF, 1090, 143A, 133F, 091C) is inferred from usage but their internal effects (animations, movement semantics) are not defined here.
077B's internal script format and the exact conditions that control its loop branches (BP-03h values) remain only partially inferred.

main_function.name: U8P_GUARD3::look
main_function.purpose: Respond to a player examining the guard: detect if the NPC is dead and produce a short context-appropriate bark/description.
entry_conditions: Event 0 (U8P_GUARD3::look) — invoked when a player or other actor examines/looks at the guard process

function_map.name: U8P_GUARD3::look
function_map.role: Handle 'U8P_GUARD3::look' examination event for the guard (produce short descriptive bark; checks dead state).

function_map.name: U8P_GUARD3::enterFastArea
function_map.role: Area-entry trigger: revive/teleport dead guard or perform cleanup and spawn patrol helper.

function_map.name: U8P_GUARD3::schedule
function_map.role: Set up periodic/scheduled behaviors by spawning helper methods with constructed timing/config parameters.

function_map.name: U8P_GUARD3::use
function_map.role: Handle direct player interaction: dialog branching, state flag updates, and spawning follow-up animations/movement.

function_map.name: U8P_GUARD3::077B
function_map.role: Patrol/movement routine: script-driven loop issuing pathfind requests and spawning animation/movement sequences.

function_map.name: Npc::isDead
function_map.role: Engine query: returns whether the NPC is in a dead state.

function_map.name: Item::bark
function_map.role: Engine: make the NPC/item produce a short spoken/text bark.

function_map.name: Npc::clrDead
function_map.role: Engine: clear the NPC 'dead' flag (revive semantics inferred).

function_map.name: Npc::clrInCombat
function_map.role: Engine: clear the NPC 'in combat' flag (make non-hostile).

function_map.name: Npc::teleport
function_map.role: Engine: teleport/reposition NPC and set visual parameters (x,y,frame,shape).

internal_calls.purpose: Spawn the patrol/movement helper when area entry occurs and the guard was not revived; drives subsequent patrol behavior.

external_calls.purpose: Determine whether to use the 'dead guardsman' vs 'guardsman' bark.
Emit the short description text to the inspecting player/process.
Check if the guard is dead to decide whether to revive/teleport it or spawn patrol helper.
Clear the dead flag when reviving a guard on area entry.
Clear combat status after reviving so the guard is non-hostile.
Reposition the revived guard to specific coordinates/appearance.
Free/unregister area-related info/resources when not reviving the guard (cleanup).
Cleanup/unregister area resources on leaving the fast-area.
Emit greetings, warnings and replies as short barks during dialog.
Spawn a small response animation/behavior at the start of dialog (inferred).
Spawn short waits/delays between dialog or animation steps.
Spawn a follow-up action after some dialog branches (likely causes the NPC to walk off or perform an action).
Introduce randomness into greeting/reply selection to vary dialog.
Present the player with a list of dialogue choices and receive the selected string for branching.
Spawn scheduled behavior callback configured by schedule locals.
Spawn an alternate scheduled callback depending on METHOD::143A's spawn result.
Request pathfinding towards patrol target coordinates in the scripted loop.
Spawn short animation/movement actions as part of patrol steps (byte-parameterized sequences).

reads.name: hydrosFree
reads.likely_effect: Used in U8P_GUARD3::use to influence which Titan/warning line is chosen; increments a local counter used for selection.

reads.name: hasHeart
reads.likely_effect: Read by U8P_GUARD3::use to distinguish undead vs other threats in dialog selection; affects the warning branch and counter.

reads.name: pyrosFree
reads.likely_effect: Used in U8P_GUARD3::use to influence warning text selection and increment the local counter.

reads.name: guard3Met
reads.likely_effect: Per-guard persistent flag read to choose greeting variants (whether the player met this guard before).

reads.name: guard3Pissed
reads.likely_effect: Per-guard flag read to determine if the guard should use an angry reply variant during use dialog.

writes.name: guard3Met
writes.likely_effect: Set to true once the player meets/queries the guard so future interactions use the 'met before' branch.

writes.name: guard3Pissed
writes.likely_effect: Set or cleared by dialog choices (e.g., 'Where am I?' can set it), influencing later dialog tone and replies.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Provides NPC (guardsman) responses to being looked at and used (talked to), manages area-trigger resurrection/cleanup, and runs a patrol/movement helper.
